channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy's loss means for upcoming Republican primaries
item.description: Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy is the latest casualty in President Trump's campaign to vanquish Republicans he sees as disloyal. What does it mean for primaries this week?
item.content:encoded: Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy is the latest casualty in President Trump's campaign to vanquish Republicans he sees as disloyal. What does it mean for primaries this week?
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Alabama march traces path of the civil rights movement while gearing up for new fight
item.description: A civil rights protest in Alabama this weekend was organized to kick off a summer of voter mobilization and civic action across the South.
item.content:encoded: A civil rights protest in Alabama this weekend was organized to kick off a summer of voter mobilization and civic action across the South.
item.dc:creator: Joseph King

item.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley, I-Calif, on his bill to stop mid-cycle redistricting efforts
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Independent Rep. Kevin Kiley of California about the implications of redistricting efforts.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Independent Rep. Kevin Kiley of California about the implications of redistricting efforts.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: The 'fibermaxxing' trend has health benefits worth the hype
item.description: There are a lot of wellness trends that make health experts roll their eyes in skepticism. But there is one big trend that many experts can get behind – with a few caveats: fibermaxxing.
item.content:encoded:
There are a lot of wellness trends that make health experts roll their eyes in skepticism. But there is one big trend that many experts can get behind – with a few caveats: fibermaxxing.

(Image credit: Natalia Gdovskaia)
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: UFC CEO Dana White on the growth of the sport and the upcoming White House bout
item.description: For NPR's Newsmakers series, Steve Inskeep speaks with Ultimate Fighting Championship CEO Dana White.
item.content:encoded: For NPR's Newsmakers series, Steve Inskeep speaks with Ultimate Fighting Championship CEO Dana White.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: How President Trump's influence might affect upcoming Republican primaries, WHO declares ebola outbreak in Africa a global health emergency, jury deliberations begin in OpenAI case.
item.content:encoded: How President Trump's influence might affect upcoming Republican primaries, WHO declares ebola outbreak in Africa a global health emergency, jury deliberations begin in OpenAI case.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Ukraine launches large-scale drone strikes on Russia
item.description: As peace talks stall, Ukraine launches one of the biggest drone strikes against Russia since the beginning of Russia's full-scale invasion in an attempt to weaken Russia's war machine.
item.content:encoded: As peace talks stall, Ukraine launches one of the biggest drone strikes against Russia since the beginning of Russia's full-scale invasion in an attempt to weaken Russia's war machine.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: North Korea is relieved its nuclear weapons have prevented a fate like Iran's
item.description: The U.S. long pushed for both Iran and North Korea to quit their nuclear plans. After seeing Iran's leader get killed, North Korea's Kim Jong Un believes he made the right choice to stay the course.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. long pushed for both Iran and North Korea to quit their nuclear plans. After seeing Iran's leader get killed, North Korea's Kim Jong Un believes he made the right choice to stay the course.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Timmy the humpback whale found dead after rescue effort
item.description: A humpback whale nicknamed Timmy, whose plight fascinated Germans, died after being towed back to the open sea in a rescue operation.
item.content:encoded: A humpback whale nicknamed Timmy, whose plight fascinated Germans, died after being towed back to the open sea in a rescue operation.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: As the federal government rolls back climate action, U.S. cities and states step up
item.description: NPR's Julia Simon kicks off Climate Solutions Week with a look at how cities and states are working to reduce emissions.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Julia Simon kicks off Climate Solutions Week with a look at how cities and states are working to reduce emissions.
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon
